mnemonics.title: JSP Processing Steps
mnemonics.content: R-P-C-I-E-R-R: 'Really Patient Cats Instantly Eat Raw Rice' (Request → Parsing → Compilation → Instantiation → Execution → Rendering → Response)

mnemonics.title: JSP Lifecycle Phases
mnemonics.content: Tom Can Light Incandescent Lamps Rightly During Dusk: Translation → Compilation → Loading → Instantiation → Initialization → Request Handling → Destruction

mnemonics.title: JSP Elements Order
mnemonics.content: Dear David, Stop Eating Cookies: Directives <%@ %> → Declarations <%! %> → Scriptlets <% %> → Expressions <%= %> → Comments <%-- -->

mnemonics.title: Implicit Objects
mnemonics.content: Real Programmers Save All Outstanding Variables Except Page Config: Request → Response → Session → Application → Out → PageContext → Exception → Config

acronyms.usage: Reminds purpose of generating dynamic web content
Simplifies data access in JSP using ${} syntax
Predefined tags for common tasks like loops and conditionals

acronyms.expansion: Client's Object Offering Key Information Exchange

visualTips.visualization: Imagine a JSP file being fed into a compiler machine that outputs a servlet .java file
Picture a Swiss Army knife with tools labeled: request, response, session, application, out
Visualize Lego blocks (tags) fitting into HTML structure to build dynamic content
A caterpillar (JSP) transforming into a butterfly (HTML), with each growth stage labeled as a phase

analogies.analogy: Like a chef (servlet) using a recipe (JSP) to prepare a dish (HTML response) with fresh ingredients (dynamic data)
Similar to a car assembly line: JSP file (raw materials) → Compiled servlet (chassis) → Initialized servlet (engine installed) → HTML output (finished car)
Like a pre-stocked toolbox - each tool (request, response, etc.) is readily available without declaration
